name: Cab Calloway
abstract: Cabell Calloway III (December 25, 1907 – November 18, 1994) was an American jazz singer, songwriter, bandleader, and actor. He was a regular performer at the Cotton Club in Harlem, where he became a popular vocalist of the swing era.

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: And how I got the gig was they went to California... Cab Calloway had to come back east without a bass player... Cab says, 'Can I have him?' So Zutty says, 'Yeah you can have him' and nobody asked me nothing. So they just gave me to Cab.
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I worked there with Cab Calloway. I worked with Cab Calloway – he only had a trio, and me. One trumpet, and him – we were doing the same numbers that he does... I’m playing trumpet with his group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: I used to stay up until twelve o’clock midnight to listen to Cab Calloway. I liked the way he sang, you know, he was a shouter. But the band was excellent.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Palmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 24, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: I was chosen to pay tribute to Cozy Cole, who was the great drummer who played with Louis Armstrong and Cab Calloway.
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: when I joined his band... I watched the other guys in the band... Then I had Cab one day to sit down and talk to me

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Ascione
about_quotes.evidence: When I got to play with Jay McShann and Cab Calloway and Al Hirt and Milt Hinton and all those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Ascione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001
